name: tamagui-best-practices
description: Provides Tamagui patterns for config v4, compiler optimization, styled context, and cross-platform styling. Must use when working with Tamagui projects (tamagui.config.ts, @tamagui imports).
capabilities: Config v4 setup with createTamagui and createThemes patterns
Compound components using createStyledContext and withStaticProperties
Compiler optimization techniques and escape hatches
Animation drivers for CSS and react-native-reanimated
Dialog, Sheet, Popover, and Select overlay patterns with Adapt
Form patterns with react-hook-form and zod validation
author: 0xBigBoss
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown files with code examples. No executable code, scripts, network calls, file system access, or external command execution. All static findings are false positives triggered by documentation patterns (backticks in code blocks, .md file extensions, curl references for fetching docs).
valueStatement: Building consistent web and native interfaces with Tamagui requires understanding compiler optimization, design tokens, and platform-specific patterns. This skill provides expert patterns for config v4, compound components, animations, and accessible overlays.
limitations: Does not replace reading official Tamagui documentation for latest API changes
Does not generate complete applications or components from scratch
Does not provide debugging or troubleshooting for Tamagui runtime errors

useCases.title: Build native-first UI
useCases.description: Create iOS and Android interfaces with Tamagui animations, sheets, and native-optimized styling

useCases.title: Share UI code across platforms
useCases.description: Maintain single codebase for web and native with compiler optimization and design tokens

useCases.title: Create accessible overlays
useCases.description: Implement Dialog, Popover, and Select components with proper accessibility and Adapt patterns

promptTemplates.title: Config setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up Tamagui config v4 with createTamagui, custom themes using createThemes, and recommended settings for React Native compatibility

promptTemplates.title: Compound component
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Button component with compound components using createStyledContext and withStaticProperties pattern

promptTemplates.title: Overlay pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a Dialog component that adapts to Sheet on touch devices using Adapt pattern

promptTemplates.title: Performance optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my component code and identify patterns that break compiler optimization

bestPractices: Use variants with spread operators instead of inline functions for compiler optimization
Apply animations to Content components, never to Portal containers
Use Adapt pattern for touch devices instead of Platform.OS branching
antiPatterns: Avoid inline style objects with dynamic values - breaks compiler extraction
Do not mix React Native StyleSheet with Tamagui tokens
Avoid creating functions in render or spreading unknown props

faq.question: How does Tamagui handle cross-platform styling?
faq.answer: Tamagui uses Adapt to transform components between platforms. Dialog becomes Sheet on touch devices automatically.

faq.question: What is the compiler optimization benefit?
faq.answer: Static props and variants are extracted at build time to atomic CSS, reducing runtime overhead significantly.

faq.question: How do I create custom themes?
faq.answer: Use createThemes with palette, accent, and childrenThemes for semantic color systems across light and dark modes.

faq.question: Which animation driver should I choose?
faq.answer: CSS driver for web (smallest bundle), react-native-reanimated for native off-thread animations.

faq.question: How do I prevent form submission issues?
faq.answer: Always wrap submit buttons in Form.Trigger with asChild. Without it, onSubmit will not fire.

faq.question: Can I use Tamagui with design systems like Storybook?
faq.answer: Yes. Document design tokens and component variants in Storybook for team consistency and testing.
